text.title: rag-eval-retrieval-metrics Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of rag-eval-retrieval-metrics enforces.

rule.statement: Report metrics at a single explicit K (default K=5). Document K in the report header.
rule.rationale: Comparing P@3 to P@10 across runs is meaningless.

rule.statement: Always include MRR and hit_rate alongside precision/recall; never report precision alone.
rule.rationale: Precision alone hides whether the correct doc was anywhere in top-K vs absent entirely.

rule.statement: When relevance is graded (not binary), include NDCG; for binary labels report only MRR + P/R.
rule.rationale: NDCG only adds information when graded relevance exists; otherwise it duplicates P@K.

rule.statement: Export per-query rows with retrieved_ids[], relevant_ids[], metric values; the aggregate is derived not stored separately.
rule.rationale: Per-query rows are reproducible; aggregates can be re-computed from them.

rule.statement: Record embedding model, index hash, top_K used, and test-set hash in the report header.
rule.rationale: Without these, cross-run comparisons are invalid.

rule.statement: Skip when the test set has no labeled relevant chunks.
rule.rationale: Stub rule referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml when none of the qualifying branches fire.
